questions.question: How long does dental bonding last?
questions.answer: With proper care, dental bonding typically lasts 5-10 years before needing touch-ups or replacement.

questions.question: Is the procedure painful?
questions.answer: No, dental bonding is usually painless and rarely requires anesthesia unless filling a cavity.

questions.question: Can bonded teeth stain?
questions.answer: Yes, composite resin can stain over time. Regular cleaning and limiting staining substances helps maintain appearance.

questions.question: How soon can I eat after bonding?
questions.answer: You can eat immediately after the procedure, but should avoid hard foods and staining substances for the first 48 hours.

reviews.name: Lisa K.
reviews.comment: Quick and painless! My chipped tooth looks perfect now - you can't even tell it was damaged.

reviews.name: James P.
reviews.comment: Affordable solution for my gap. The whole process took less than an hour!
